title: Nan-e Berenji (Rice Cookies)
description: Delicate, melt-in-your-mouth Persian rice flour cookies infused with rose water and cardamom, traditionally adorned with a stamp pattern and poppy seeds.

instructions.text: In a large bowl, beat the softened clarified butter (ghee) and powdered sugar until light and fluffy.
Add the egg yolk and mix until fully combined.
Stir in the rose water and cardamom powder.
Gradually add the fine rice flour, kneading gently until a soft, smooth dough forms. Do not overmix. Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest at room temperature for 1 to 2 hours.
Preheat your oven to 325°F (160°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

Roll the dough into small, walnut-sized balls. Place them on the baking sheet about 1 inch apart. Flatten slightly, and press a cookie stamp or a thimble onto the top of each cookie to create a traditional pattern.
Sprinkle a tiny pinch of poppy seeds in the center of each cookie.
Bake for 12-15 minutes. The cookies should remain pale and not turn brown. Let them cool completely on the baking sheet before moving, as they are very fragile when warm.

tips: It is critical to use extremely fine rice flour (asian market style). If your dough is too crumbly after resting, add a few drops of water or extra rose water.
storage_notes: Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 weeks.

substitutions.note: Can be pressed into the center for a different look and crunch.
Shortening keeps the cookies pure white, though ghee adds better flavor.
